[…] Pollution with polychlorinated biphenyls (PCBs), dioxins, and furan in breast milk cannot be avoided; only a legal ban would lead to a reduction in these pollutants.To reduce the fear of hospitals in children, in 1994 the Children’s Hospital Glanzing established a yearly summer children’s festival before the end of the school term, with more than 2,500 parents and children attending in 2007.
32938229 India has highest estimated lip and oral cavity cancer cases worldwide (119,992, 33.8%) and the secondhighest number of breast (162,468, 17.8%) and cervix uteri (96,922,30.7%) cancers in Asian sub-continent.
32939636 The protocol required 20 cores of the liver, 30 of lung, 12 of spleen, 20 of heart, 20 of kidney, 4 of breast, 4 of testis, 2 of skeletal muscle, and 4 of fat with total of 112 cores per patient.
32947599 Regarding type of feeding, 41.7% of the newborns received exclusive breast-feeding at discharge and 40.4% at 1 month. […] Exclusive breast-feeding rates at discharge and at 1 month of age were lower than expected.
32948493 This article examines the current evidence to assess the feasibility of recommencing immediate breast reconstruction services during the pandemic and highlights considerations required to ensure patient safety.
32948989 These issues are best addressed through the application of currently available medical technology and enhanced training in advanced oncoplastic techniques, which extend the limits of breast conservation.
32956343 The following conditions were included in the scope of this review: gynecological tumors, breast cancer, gastrointestinal tumors, genitourinary tumors, head and neck tumors, skin cancer, tumors of the central nervous system, and lymphomas.
32958531 Most patients (190, 40.8%) had breast cancer, and chemotherapy with or without immunotherapy was administered in 323 (69.3%) patients.
32966801 Viral RNA was detected in breast milk in at least seven studies, raising the possibility that ACE2 is expressed in mammary tissue during lactation.
32970384 There was relative increase in breast and lymph node samples; however, this relative increase was not statistically significant. […] Breast samples remain the most frequent sample type both in pre-COVID-19 and COVID-19 periods.
32972763 In this review, we report a case of a bone’s metastatic breast cancer in Malian patient treated by chemotherapy in whom SRAS-COV-2’s diagnosis was made 9days after the onset gastrointestinal symptoms.
32982846 The Coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) outbreak generated an unprecedented set of emotional challenges for women diagnosed with breast cancer. […] Women diagnosed with primary breast cancer (N = 234) completed a series of online questionnaires to assess their cognitive and emotional wellbeing as well as their COVID-EMV. […] Our findings indicate that concerns about COVID-19 amongst women affected by breast cancer leads to increased risk of developing affective disorder, such as anxiety and depression symptomatology, among this sample. […] We advocate the rapid implementation of accessible interventions designed to promote emotional resilience in the breast cancer population.
32986489 Background: In the current study, we reported our experience on sentinel node mapping of breast cancer patients during the extreme shortage of Mo99-Tc99m generators using Tc-99m phytate.
32989430 We aimed to explore the treatment changes and delays of early breast cancer (EBC) during the first quarter of 2020. […] Only 5·2% of breast cancer diagnosis occurred after quarantine in Hubei compared with 15·3% in other provinces (OR= 0·30, 95%CI 0·24-0·38). postoperative endocrine therapy were least affected compared with different regions after quarantine (OR=0·37 [95%CI 0·19-0·73]).
32992777 We assessed the inhibition of important efflux (P-glycoprotein (P-gp), breast cancer resistance protein (BCRP)) and uptake transporters (organic anion transporting polypeptide (OATP)-1B1, OATP1B3, OATP2B1) by hydroxychloroquine, tested its P-gp and BCRP substrate characteristics, and evaluated the induction of pharmacokinetically relevant genes regulated by the nuclear pregnane X (PXR) (CYP3A4, ABCB1) and aryl hydrocarbon receptor (AhR) (CYP1A1, CYP1A2).
32998572 Maternal bonding was defined by events such as rooming-in, skin to skin contact (STSC), and breastfeeding. […] From the remaining 26 (84%), 17 (65%) opted for rooming-in, 12 (46%) for STSC, and 16 (61%) fed the infants with breastmilk (11 direct breastfeedings and five pumped the breast milk).
33006676 The majority (66.4%) of patients were women and the most common disease was breast cancer (40%).
33010487 All patients with non-metastatic breast cancer or gynecological cancer were included.
33010487 All patients with non-metastatic breast cancer or gynecological cancer were included.
33010487 All patients with non-metastatic breast cancer or gynecological cancer were included.
33010487 All patients with non-metastatic breast cancer or gynecological cancer were included.
33014800 Social media were the second most common source of knowledge (43.8%) with a predilection in younger patients, nonsmokers, targeted therapy- or immunotherapy-treated patients, and breast cancer patients (>65 vs. <65 years of age, p = 0.057; nonsmoker vs. ever-smoker, p = 0.036; targeted therapy and immunotherapy vs. chemotherapy, p = 0.004; breast cancer vs. other cancers, p = 0.019).
33015560 One of the major concerns is the tendency for delayed surgical treatment of breast cancer patients. […] Here in this paper, we report the results of a consensus procedure which aimed to provide an expert opinion-led guideline for breast cancer management during the COVID-19 outbreak in Turkey. […] We used the Delphi method with a 9-scale Likert scale on two rounds of voting from 51 experienced surgeons and medical oncologists who had the necessary skills and experience in breast cancer management.
33015823 The most common cancer type was breast cancer.
33017762 The assessment revealed that hormetic effects have been commonly induced by chloroquine, affecting numerous cell types, including tumor cell lines (e.g. human breast and colon) and non-tumor cell lines, enhancing viral replication, sperm motility, various behavioral endpoints as well as decreasing risks of convulsions, and enhancing a spectrum of neuroprotective responses within a preconditioning experimental framework.
33026711 The first wave of the COVID-19 pandemic in Australia forced a temporary closure of BreastScreen Australia services. […] Now reopened, the BreastScreen experience has been redefined for both staff and clients and the journey to the ‘new BreastScreen normal’ is continually evolving in response to the ongoing threat of COVID-19 and government directives on health policy. […] Beyond the pandemic, the ‘new normal’ needs to be one which enables well women to continue screening with a visible sense of reassurance that all that can be done is being done to ensure the safe and continued early detection of breast cancer.
33033166 To assess the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on the rate of newly diagnosed gynecological and breast cancers in Austria. […] Data of 2077 patients from 18 centers in Austria with newly diagnosed gynecological or breast cancer between January and May 2019 and January and May 2020 were collected. […] The lockdown led to a decreased number of newly diagnosed gynecological and breast cancers.
33036896 The Accreditation Council for Graduate Medical Education (ACGME) Core Competencies for Diagnostic Radiology helped guide division leadership in restructuring and reframing breast imaging education during this time of drastic change and persistent uncertainty. […] Here, we reflect on the educational challenges and opportunities faced by our academic breast imaging division during the height of the COVID-19 pandemic across each of the ACGME Core Competencies.
33036897 Mean volume decrease differed amongst divisions: Interventional Radiology experienced least disruption (29% volume decrease), 7 divisions experienced 40%-60% decreases, and Musculoskeletal, Breast, and Cardiovascular imaging experienced >75% volume decrease.
33037572 Given her travel history to the epidemic area and the chest CT scan results, the patient was immediately admitted to the isolation ward of the Infectious Disease Department and breastfeeding was discontinued. […] During hospitalization, she also experienced bilateral breast tenderness. […] The management of mother-child interactions and breastfeeding in women with COVID-19 is a difficult problem.
33038477 Delivery mode, skin-to-skin contact and breastfeeding initiation did not differ between groups. […] Reflecting younger infant age, 59% of DL infants were exclusively breast-fed/mixed fed versus 39% of BL (p < 0.05). 13% reported a change in feeding; often related to lack of breastfeeding support, especially with practical problems.
33043705 Male and female patients had a high percentage of prostate (39.3%) and breast (32%) cancer respectively.
33047649 A number of critical issues have come out during COVID-19 pandemic in the management of patients with metastatic breast cancer (MBC). […] To explore the changes in the treatment of patients with MBC during COVID-19 pandemic, we promoted a survey to the oncologists operating in the Italian breast units.
